calendar.matters:
Motion For Apportionment And Distribution Of Funds
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Motion for Apportionment and Distribution of Funds.
Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy, Clerk: J. Bertelsen. Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq. appeared for the Plaintiff Debbie Bess as wrongful death heir and as successor-in-interest to Bobby Bess, deceased; and Bobby Wayne Bess and Tony A. Killgore as legal heirs of Bobby Bess, deceased ("plaintiffs").

No appearance by Plaintiffs Debbie Bess, Bobby Wayne Bess, and Tony A. Killgore.
After reviewing the moving paers and considering the evidence both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, the Court ordered for distribution of all net settlements funds derived from plaintiffs' survival/wrongful death cause of action as following proportions:
Estate of Bobby Bess 0%
Debbie Bess 25%
Bobby Wayne Bess 25%
Tonya Redpath 25%
Natasha Bess 25%

The Signature of plaintiff Debbie Bess alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.
Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for order to apportion and distribute funds will be heard at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514, Hon. Stephen M. Murphy presiding. Henceforth, all asbestos apportionment motions will be heard in Department 514 and such motions shall be filed in accordance with San Francisco Uniform Local Rules of Court, Rule 8.1(B)(5). Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Matter Not Reported. (503/CML)

MOTION To Dismiss Or Stay On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
SARA M. PARKER, ESQ.(MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP), (415)267-4117, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, DEBBIE BESS

THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION To Dismiss Or Stay On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO STAY ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON Conveniens ONLY. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (301/PJB)
